suggested_cluster_label: Thin-film solid-state battery fabrication via sputter deposition
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (electrode, electrolyte, cathode, substrate, layer, cell, plasma, deposition, target, battery) and title terms (energy storage, sputter deposition, cathode, composition) consistently point to thin-film energy storage devices. Representative titles ('ENERGY STORAGE DEVICE', 'STACK FOR AN ENERGY STORAGE DEVICE', 'METHOD OF MAKING A COMPONENT FOR A SOLID-STATE ELECTROCHEMICAL CELL') and abstracts describing first/second electrode layers with an electrolyte layer between them on a substrate directly support a solid-state electrochemical cell stack built by deposition. Sputter/plasma + target terminology supports the deposition-process module.

suggested_cluster_label: Haircare appliances with airflow-based styling attachments
engineering_module_candidates: Airflow generation subsystem (air inlet, air outlet, airflow generator)
Interchangeable styling attachments (e.g., diffuser, handheld attachment)
Attachment aerodynamic surfaces (curved/flat surfaces downstream of air outlet)
rationale: Top terms ('haircare', 'haircare appliance', 'styling', 'airflow', 'attachment', 'air inlet', 'air outlet') and repeated titles 'HAIRCARE APPLIANCE' indicate a dominant theme of air-driven hair styling devices. Abstracts explicitly describe an airflow generator routing air from inlet to outlet, and attachments with curved/flat surfaces downstream of the outlet, supporting both the airflow subsystem and attachment modules. The title term 'diffuser' supports an interchangeable attachment category.

suggested_cluster_label: Handheld fluid-flow appliances (e.g., hairdryers) with internal duct/flow-path architecture
engineering_module_candidates: Internal duct and fluid flow path
rationale: Representative titles are all 'HAND HELD APPLIANCE' and abstracts repeatedly describe a hairdryer-like device with a body, duct, fluid inlet, fluid outlet, and a primary fluid flow path. Top terms ('fluid', 'fluid flow path', 'fluid inlet', 'fluid outlet', 'wall', 'duct') support body/duct and inlet/outlet modules; title terms 'heater assembly', 'attachment', and 'cleaning appliance' support heater and attachment modules. The 'cleaning appliance' term appears only in title terms with no abstract evidence here, so that scope is uncertain.

suggested_cluster_label: Sensor-driven smart vacuum cleaners and mobile cleaning robots
rationale: Top terms 'vacuum', 'vacuum motor', 'vacuum cleaner', 'sensor', 'controller', 'mobile robot' and titles dominated by 'VACUUM CLEANER' and 'mobile robot' indicate a cleaning-appliance focus. Abstracts repeatedly describe a vacuum motor, motion/orientation sensors generating signals, diagnostic sensors, an agitator-equipped cleaner head, and a controller that processes signals via a surface-type model—supporting the listed modules. Terms 'oral' and 'hairstyling' appear in titles but are not reflected in abstracts, so they are treated as minor outliers and excluded from module suggestions.

suggested_cluster_label: Vacuum Cleaner Head with Agitator/Brush Bar
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cleaner head', 'agitator', 'brush bar', 'suction', 'housing', 'chamber') and the dominant representative title 'CLEANER HEAD FOR A VACUUM CLEANER' indicate the cluster centers on vacuum cleaner head designs. Abstracts explicitly describe housings defining suction chambers with inlets/outlets, single or dual agitator elements mounted for rotation, and drive mechanisms about specific axes, supporting the housing, agitator, and drive modules. One abstract referencing a 'laser diode for illuminating debris' supports an illumination sub-module, though this appears in fewer examples so its prominence is lower.

suggested_cluster_label: Surface treating appliance main body & support/wand architecture
rationale: Top terms ('main body', 'support assembly', 'surface treating head', 'hose', 'wand', 'appliance') and abstracts repeatedly describe vacuum-cleaner-type surface treating appliances composed of a main body, a rollably mounted support assembly, a surface treating head, and a hose/wand assembly with a handle. The 'robotic' title term appears but is not strongly supported by the abstracts, so it is omitted from modules.

suggested_cluster_label: Multistage cyclonic dirt/dust separation for vacuum cleaners
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms ('cyclonic separating', 'cyclone', 'dirt', 'dust', 'separator', 'chamber', 'stage', 'vacuum cleaner') and titles like 'MULTISTAGE CYCLONIC SEPARATING APPARATUS' and 'Dirt and Dust Cyclonic Separating Apparatus' point to cyclonic separation technology. Abstracts explicitly describe first and second cyclonic separating units with annular dust collecting chambers and an airflow path from dirty air inlet to clean air outlet, supporting the listed sub-system modules. Handheld cleaning appliance titles support the vacuum housing integration module.

engineering_module_candidates: Nozzle airflow module (interior passage and mouth for emitting air)
Base impeller-motor module (air inlet, impeller, motor driving air to outlet)
rationale: Top terms ('fan assembly', 'nozzle', 'interior passage', 'mouth', 'air outlet', 'impeller', 'receiving air') and abstracts describing a bladeless fan with a base housing an impeller/motor and a nozzle with an interior passage and mouth strongly indicate bladeless nozzle-type fan assemblies. The title term 'humidifying' supports a humidification variant as a sub-module, though it appears less frequently so confidence on that module is moderate.

engineering_module_candidates: AC-to-DC rectification stage for motor drive
rationale: Top terms ('brushless motor', 'winding', 'voltage', 'control', 'phase winding', 'current', 'period') and representative titles ('CONTROL OF A BRUSHLESS MOTOR', 'CONTROL OF A BRUSHLESS PERMANENT-MAGNET MOTOR') consistently point to brushless/PM motor control. Abstracts explicitly describe exciting motor windings, freewheeling when winding current exceeds a threshold or after a timeout, and rectifying an AC voltage to drive the winding—supporting the excitation/freewheeling, rectification, and phase-winding drive modules.

engineering_module_candidates: Rotor assembly (shaft, rotor core, magnet, impeller)
Stator assembly (stator core, C-shaped core variants)
rationale: Top TF-IDF terms (rotor, stator, bearing, shaft, frame, stator core, impeller, adhesive) and title terms (brushless motor, compressor, turbomachine) consistently point to brushless motor construction. Representative titles ('BRUSHLESS MOTOR', 'COMPRESSOR', 'BRUSHLESS MOTOR AND STATOR THEREFOR') and abstracts repeatedly describe a rotor assembly (shaft, rotor core, bearing assembly, impeller), a stator assembly (including C-shaped stator core), and a frame housing both — supporting the listed modules. Mention of 'compressor' and 'impeller' supports a turbomachine integration sub-module.

suggested_cluster_label: Cleaning appliances (floor tools and dental fluid-jet cleaners)
engineering_module_candidates: Floor tool / cleaner head for vacuum cleaning appliance
Handle and detachable cleaning tool interface
Working fluid reservoir and delivery conduit
Nozzle assembly for fluid burst delivery (dental)
rationale: Top terms ('cleaning appliance', 'floor', 'floor tool', 'vacuum cleaning appliance', 'suction') and titles repeatedly featuring 'CLEANING APPLIANCE' indicate floor-tool/vacuum cleaning subsystems, while abstracts consistently describe a 'dental cleaning appliance' with 'handle', 'nozzle', 'working fluid', 'fluid reservoir', and 'fluid conduit', supporting fluid-delivery modules. The cluster appears to mix two cleaning-appliance domains (vacuum floor tools and dental fluid-jet devices), so module candidates are drawn from both; confidence is moderate due to this mixed evidence.

suggested_cluster_label: Air treatment devices (wearable air purifiers and hand dryers)
engineering_module_candidates: Wearable ear/headset assembly with integrated speakers
rationale: Top terms ('filter', 'air', 'airflow', 'fan', 'wearable', 'purifier', 'hand dryer', 'drying') and representative titles ('HEAD WEARABLE AIR PURIFIER', 'AIR TREATMENT APPARATUS') indicate air-moving products. Abstracts explicitly describe a filter assembly, motor-driven impeller generating airflow, and ear/speaker assemblies, supporting the wearable air purifier modules. The recurring 'hand dryer' and 'drying' terms in TF-IDF suggest a secondary hand-dryer sub-theme, though it is less represented in the sampled titles/abstracts (lower confidence for that sub-module).
